observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds
and Due to Other Funds and effect adjustments to reflect the correct
account balances and fair presentation in the financial statements,
pursuant to Sections 111 and 112 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a) Municipal Internal Auditor to recommend internal control over the
procurement, storage and issuance of supplies and materials; and
b) Municipal Accountant/Supply Officer/General Service Officer/
Inventory Committee to: (i) ensure that Supplies Ledger Cards and
Stock Cards are maintained accurately and updated regularly; and
(ii) conduct semestral physical inventory counts and reconcile the
results with the accounting records.

We further recommended to provide adequate training to personnel
responsible for maintaining inventory records and conducting of
physical inventories.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the HRMO to
submit any changes in the payroll to the GSIS every month so that the
GSIS system can capture the updated data hence, accurate computation
of the premiums, and loans obtained can be arrived and reflected in the
billing statements and the corresponding payroll deductions and
remittances of the same could be made accurately.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the Municipal
Treasurer and other officials concerned to adequately insure all
insurable properties of the LGU with the GIF of GSIS as protection
against their loss or damage due to fortuitous events. Likewise, the same
offices should collaborate in identifying other serviceable and insurable
properties and in the preparation of Inventory of all insurable properties
for submission to GIF of the GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO in
coordination with Sangguniang Bayan Members to come up with
concrete policy guidelines concerning the hiring and rehiring of Casual
personnel to establish a “clear expectation”.

We further recommended that upon hiring of Casual personnel, they
should be provided with a Statement of Duties and Responsibilities to
clearly identify their responsibilities and perform their duties
efficiently.

Also, we recommended that the HRMO should set a standardized wage
rate based on their qualifications and suitability for the job applied for.

We recommended for the LCE the following courses of action:
• Require the HR
  a) To determine the necessity, assess the duties and functions,
     taking into account their expertise, experience to handle the
     position in order to come up with a recommendation as to the
     ideal number of personnel to be hired; and
  b) To submit duly accomplished and signed DTR and other
     documents prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and to
     assign a timekeeper for those in the fields to check on the
     attendance or services rendered.
• Require the Municipal Accountant to identify the causes of the late
  payment of the wages of job order employees and to devise a system
  to rectify such deficiency/ies.

We further recommended that an authority from the sanggunian be
secured in the hiring of JOs pursuant to Sec. 77 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to
attach the necessary supporting documents before payment pursuant to
Sec 4 (6) of PD 1445.

We further recommended that a municipal ordinance be passed to
allocate funds for the payment of cash assistance to barangay tanods, to
serve as an authority to pay pursuant to Section 393 of RA 7160.
Likewise, the CAFGU’s residency and their status as in active service
should be established, or else stop granting cash assistance to the
personnel mentioned to safeguard the funds from possible wastage.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal
Person and Department Heads concerned to:
a) Endeavor to fully implement the programs/projects/activities
contained in its GAD Plan and Budget to achieve its intended
purposes; and
b) Adhere to the DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget in the
implementation of the programs and utilization of funds
incorporated therein.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor and the members of the
Local School Board the following courses of action:
a) Ensure that the date of provision of a copy of the SEF Budget to the
Local Budget Officer, Local Accountant and Local Treasurer be
appropriately logged;
b) Submit promptly the quarterly and annual reports to the DepEd CO,
copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and regional offices of
the DBM and DILG within the timeline; and
c) Monitor the posting of utilization report of SEF budget, pursuant to
the Full Disclosure Policy of the DILG.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enforce the following
courses of action:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to issue memorandum to the
defaulting RCCs to turn over the AFs with the corresponding amount of
collections duly noted by the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal
Accountant to send demand letters to the AOs concerned to produce
the Accountable Forms and to prepare the corresponding RCDs and
RAAFs immediately;
b) Require both Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
establish the accountability of the said AOs and demand the
production of cash corresponding to their collections;
c) Enforce legal remedy to recover the funds entrusted to them; and
d) Impose appropriate sanctions applicable in the circumstances.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive the following courses of
action:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
review the supporting documents before payment, and
b) Ensure that the payments made to the legitimate payee is based on the
supporting documents.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive the following courses of
action:
a) Require the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that the contract
awardees for the procurement of goods understand and comply with
the terms and conditions provided in the contract. Further, consider
in the post-qualification of bids and evaluation of received quotations,
for purposes of awarding the contracts, the performance and
compliance to contract terms of bidder-supplier in past transactions.
Moreover, make sure that the Purchase Order and the accompanying
supporting documents should be properly accomplished to show
relevant information; and
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that penalties resulting
from delays in the delivery of goods by suppliers are computed and to
deduct any money due or which may become due to them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive take the following
courses of action:
a) Direct the BAC Chairperson to ensure that the 25% of the monthly
basic salary ceiling is imposed in the computation of honorarium of
the BAC that is based on the number of completed procurement
projects in order to avoid any overpayment; and
b) Stop granting Honoraria to those who did not actually attend to the
conduct of BAC meetings or proceedings.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and verify the official receipts submitted by the RCCs to accurately report the collections as they occur; b) Require the RCCs to present the AFs mentioned in Table 4 or present any proof of remittance so that proper adjustments could be made; and c) Effect the adjustments on the TF books as a result of the reconciliation made between the Accounting and Treasurers records.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments are made by the Municipal Accountant to reflect the reconciliation.
observations.reason: Lack of time and personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to verify and validate the dormant accounts from any available source documents and draw the necessary adjusting entry if it was a result of an accounting error, or if warranted, include the accounts in the request for write-off in the following year, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: A request for write-off was provided by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and fair presentation of the assets and liabilities in the financial statements, pursuant to Sections 111 and 112 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the 'Due from' and 'Due to' accounts to come up with reconciled accounts is on the process but there were also some priority reports to adhere but it made sure that it will be reconciled until Dec 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards to monitor issuances of the inventories. Also, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to: a) Maintain stock cards, and direct the Supply Officer to submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), together with the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and other pertinent supporting documents, to the Accounting Office for recognition of expense of issued/distributed inventories; and b) Conduct periodic physical count of the inventory items, prepare and submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant to come up with an accurate recording and determination of the Inventories account balance.
observations.managementAction: Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards will ensure to maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards. The Inventory Committee has conducted an Inventory of PPEs as of June 2025 and will be consolidated by the Supply/Property Officer. The Inventory Committee has conducted the ONE TIME CLEANSING of PPE and the Inventory Committee and Accounting Office is still in the process of reconciling the accounts to ensure that all has been paid and remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive take the following courses of action: a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments to correct the misstatements found in Advances to Contractors and other related accounts; b) Exert extra efforts to locate the pertinent documents and establish the outstanding balances, and issue demand letters requiring them to return the unrecouped advances; and c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to initiate request for write-off, if necessary, on the unrecouped balance of ₱3,080.34 which had been outstanding for more than 10 years.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Local Chief Executive to take the following courses of action: a) Enjoin the Inventory Committee to include all PPE items in the physical count and require the Municipal Treasurer/GSO that property records, including the RPCPPE, be updated based on the result thereof, and reconcile the RPCPPE with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts; and b) Reconstitute the Appraisal and Disposal Committee and direct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to initiate the process of disposal of unserviceable properties and submit the pertinent documents to the Accounting Office as basis to derecognize the mentioned unserviceable properties from the books of accounts, pursuant to existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee will conduct the ONE TIME CLEANSING of PPE so that a report of PPEs will be reflected in the book of accounts and PPE will dispose the unserviceable PPEs. The Inventory Committee and Accounting Office is still in the process of reconciling the accounts and finalizing the reports to be submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action: a) Submit the incidence report of malnutrition and incidence of hunger to justify the implementation of the program; b) Require the MSWDO to submit a Supplemental Feeding Plan with budgetary requirements for each activity, the delivery schedule and the items to be delivered on a particular date shall be known to the supplier and the DCWs; and c) Document the actual delivery and transfer to each DCC. Henceforth, if the program is continued: (i) Stop the delivery during Saturdays or non-working day; (ii) Provide the necessary transportation expenses and incidental expenses and condiments; and, (iii) Calculate the quantity of food items to be purchased on factual basis.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO complied and submitted the documents required by COA and stop the implementation of this Supplemental Feeding Program because the period was shortened and done when the children were already on summer vacation. The Municipal Mayor will stop the implementation of this program. The MSWDO will submit the required supporting documents. The Delivery Schedule was indicated in the supporting documents though some delivery were reported by each Day Care Center and the distribution list is inaccurate in terms of the quantity delivered and those that were reported by each Day Care Center.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive take the following courses of action: a) Stop charging ineligible expenditure items from the 20% Development Fund and ensure that the development projects be optimally utilized for the attainment of desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU; b) Fund for PPAs which are not procurement-and-implementation-ready be realigned to other priority PPAs which could be readily procured or implemented; c) Enjoin the Local Finance Committee to reevaluate the current PPAs, more particularly on the corresponding expenditure items funded under the 20% Development Fund, and recommend reversion and transfer of ineligible PPAs, if possible, to the appropriate funds; and d) Direct the MPDC to review all its plans and programs to ensure that what is planned and programmed is in compliance with the proper charging of the said fund.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee identified the projects which remains unimplemented and declared it as savings so that it will be reappropriated to continuing the priority projects thru the Supplemental AIP and Supplemental Budget for FY 2025. The LFC has identified the projects which remains unimplemented and recommend for reversion of funds and these will be reappropriated to continue the priority projects. The Planning Office is not productive longer due to the absence of the MPDC head. The LCE will review all plans and programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive take the following courses of action: a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation documents immediately to the Municipal Accounting Unit and COA on the cash advances granted to her so that proper adjustments of her accountability on the books of accounts can be effected; and b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters periodically to enforce the immediate liquidation/settlement of past due cash advances of all concerned accountable officer and personnel and enforce strict compliance with the rules and regulations on cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters regularly periodically to enforce the immediate liquidation/settlement of past due cash advances of the concerned accountable officer and personnel and enforce strict compliance with the rules and regulations on cash advances. The Municipal Accountant prepared a random cash count of the RCCs to verify the receipts. The Municipal Treasurer was requested to submit liquidations immediately to the Municipal Accounting Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive take the following courses of action: a) Request from the SB members to ratify a well-defined policy guideline on the grant of assistance to come up with a resolution in consonance with the guidelines issued by the DSWD; b) Municipal Accountant to ascertain the completeness of the required supporting documents based on the guidelines; and c) Direct the MSWDO to prepare and submit MATRIX for the AICS program based on DSWD guidelines as an aid to the SB in the promulgation of well-defined and specific guidelines that would be consistently applied in the grant of financial assistance. Likewise, require her to maintain objectivity, in determining the type, kind, and amount of financial assistance to be recommended for approval of the appropriate authority.
observations.managementAction: The SB members ratified a well-defined policy guideline on the grant of assistance. Municipal Accountant ensures that the supporting documents were attached. The MSWDO prepared and submitted the AICS MATRIX.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) Enforce the immediate liquidation and/or refund of the unliquidated cash advances and to refrain from granting additional cash advance to officials and employees with outstanding balances until the previous cash advances are fully settled /liquidated; b) Designate a staff to closely monitor the liquidation of the outstanding cash advances; and c) Send demand letters to the employees concerned and require the submission of liquidation documents or refund the amount, whichever is applicable in the circumstances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters and designated a staff to monitor unliquidated cash advances. The Municipal Accountant enforces no cash advance if there is outstanding cash advance and issues demand letters to liquidate outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Local Chief Executive to: a) Strictly prohibit the practice of open burning of solid waste and establishment/operation of an open dump site within the municipality, and impose the corresponding sanctions/penalties in case of violation thereto; and b) Enjoin all barangay Solid Waste Management (SWM) Committees: (i) to establish their MRF in a barangay-owned or leased land or any suitable open space to be determined by the barangay through its Sanggunian; and, (ii) design the MRFs in such a way that it can receive, sort, process and store compostable and recyclable material efficiently and in an environmentally sound manner.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO will strictly prohibit the practice of open burning of solid wastes and establishment/operation of an open dump site within the municipality, and impose the corresponding sanctions/penalties. All barangay SWM Committees were enjoined to establish MRFs and some barangays have established MRFs in accordance with RA 9003.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer to make a reconciliation of the balances of the Payables Account before paying the same to avoid over/underpayment of the obligations, henceforth establish with certainty the obligations to provide reliable information in the financial statements as provided in Item no. 29 of PPSAS 1, Volume 1 of PPSAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Payroll Maker will reconcile regularly the accounts to ensure that all has been recorded paid and remitted. The Municipal Accountant and Payroll Maker will reconcile the accounts regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to update the computation of Real Property Tax based on the RPTAR/Certified list of Real Property Tax and furnish the Accounting Office the Certified list required in Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: RPTAR were updated and the RPTAR were the basis in the computation of Real Property Tax. The Municipal Treasurer updated the RPTAR and furnished the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to: a) Require the Revenue Collecting Clerks to remit the collection had by them, prepare and submit the corresponding report of collections and deposits thereon, promptly, to avoid the accumulation of cash on hand; b) Order the RCC to refrain from lending money to the officers and employees and remit the collections daily before the close of working hours; c) Admonish the RCC to observe the prescribed time frame in the remittance of the collections as provided in Sec 29 of the NGAS Manual in order to avoid accumulation of cash collections in the hands of the AOs; and d) Prohibit the RCCs to avoid encashment of checks out of collections as this is not allowed by law.
observations.managementAction: The MTO will conduct a random cash count of the RCCs and verify if the receipts to verify if the report is accurate. The RCCs remitted partially the collections and some remittances were late and made after the exit conference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, the HRMO, and the Local Finance Committee to attach/submit the duly certified copy of the Job Order Contracts/Service Contracts by the HRMO with the statement of the expected work or output; with the names of the supervisor/timekeeper, the office where the employees should report; and enforce compliance.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-HRMO will conduct a meeting on how to improve the processing of payment of wages to Job Order employees and the preparation of Job Order Contracts. The newly designated HRMO was informed about the recommendations and these will be taken action immediately.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to: (a) ensure that Cashbooks are maintained for each fund, including cashbook for her cash advances; (b) update and balance the collections, deposits, and disbursements on her cashbooks on a daily basis; (c) ensure correctness and accuracy of entries recorded in the cashbooks; and (d) make available the records for reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared the Cashbooks but not updated the reconciliation. The Municipal Accountant will conduct random cash count verifies the AFs remitted to her by the RCCs by affixing her signature on the back of the AF issued. The Liquidating Officer was able to present the AFs in Table IV and it was made after the exit conference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: a) Direct the Supply Officer/Property Custodian to adopt an appropriate documentation on the release of LGU supplies, maintain the prescribed registries and prepare the reports; b) Require the MSWDO, LDRRMO, MHO and other concerned offices to provide the Supply Officer copies of RIS/Acknowledgment Receipt and Issuance Form for in-kind donations; and c) Observe proper documentation and reporting process in the receipt and issuance of the In-Kind Donations received by the LGU and ensure that the report is sufficient in detail to facilitate proper recording and accounting. Donations directly received by the MHO from other agencies should be reported to the Property Custodian for proper recording.
observations.managementAction: The Property Officer has designated Mr Mar Jenneil Cal as the Property officer and has designated the IS and has maintained the registeries for In Kind Donations and the concerned offices were instructed to provide the RIS to the Property Officer. The Property Officer has maintained the registeries and has designated the Supply Officer to maintain the registries and prepare the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer to fully implement the PPAs in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and strengthen the capacities of the LGU in mitigating and preparing for, responding to, and recovering from the impact of disasters.
observations.managementAction: The DRRM Office already includes the thematic areas in the Investment Plan and the LDRRMO Office is now staffed with the required permanent items for the DRRM Office: (1) MDRRM O – SG 22; (2) SG 18; (3) Clerk II SG 4; (4) LDRRM Assistant SG 8. Positions were already created and filled in.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Local Chief Executive to direct the HRMO to ensure that seminars/trainings/conventions attended by its officials and employees is responsive to the organizational result and development outcomes as embodied in the Capability Development Agenda.
observations.managementAction: The Internal Audit Service thru the Internal Auditor created an Internal Guideline on the processing of Request for Travel Order, which includes checking of Outstanding Cash Advance, Availability of Funds and inclusion in the Capability Agenda of the Office Concerned. The IAS created internal guideline and the processing of Travel Orders will follow the internal rule on the processing of Travel Order.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enforce the following courses of action: a) Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the concerned AOs to produce the Accountable Forms and to prepare the corresponding RCDs and RAAFs immediately; b) Require both Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to establish the accountability of the said AOs and demand the production of cash corresponding to their collections; c) Enforce legal remedy to recover the funds entrusted to them; and d) Impose appropriate sanctions applicable in the circumstances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer issued memoranda to the defaulting RCCs to present the AFs and present the corresponding amount, the Municipal Accountant will send demand letter to the defaulting RCCs and required them to remit the collections; the defaulting RCCs remitted partially the collections. A final demand letter will be issued by Municipal Mayor to the defaulting RCCs to present the AFs with the corresponding amount, then a response to that, the amount is presumed to be in their custody and the Municipal Accountant will issue a demand letter to request liquidation or refund for the outstanding balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: a) Refrain or limit the practice of serving meals and snacks during meetings, particularly those events which are considered regular or normal activities of the LGU, exercise prudence in utilizing the funds which are intended for public purposes; and henceforth ensure the submission of documents necessary to support the validity and propriety of the meal expenses; b) Review the expenditures for catering services and ensure that the same directly supports the objective of the LGU; c) Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to evaluate the economic advantage of negotiating and awarding the catering contracts of the LGU to a single supplier; d) Conduct the bidding on piecemeal or as the need arises instead of a one-time bidding for the semester making the contract a blanket authority for all procurement for catering services; and e) Require the submission of the menu together with the actual list of food served and pictures if possible.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will strictly adhere to the recommendations and the Municipal Accountant will ensure completeness of the supporting documents attached in the billing of the Caterer. The Municipal Treasurer will ensure that only necessary events are served meals and the processing of Catering Services will be done per activity and the Bids and Awards Committee will avoid award to a single supplier for the whole quarter.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to stop charging membership and similar fees for personal or individual membership in a private organization against government funds.
observations.managementAction: Membership fees for organization is not charged to the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resources and Management Officer to strictly observe the mandatory monthly minimum take home pay threshold by refraining from accommodating salary deductions for private lending institutions and ensure compliance with the law.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant follows strictly the policy that no cash advance if there is outstanding cash advance and the payroll maker will verify the minimum take home pay for each employee and ensure compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters periodically to enforce the immediate liquidation/settlement of past due cash advances of all concerned accountable officers and personnel and enforce strict compliance with the rules and regulations on cash advances. Require the active personnel to submit their liquidation documents or refund the money, whichever is applicable in the circumstances. Further, require an Accountant’s certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books as part of the supporting documents. Likewise, refrain from granting cash advances to the national government offices or officers not connected with the municipality in order to safeguard the government funds from possible losses or misuse.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters and the Municipal Accountant prepared random cash counts of the RCCs to verify receipts. The Municipal Treasurer was requested to submit liquidation immediately to the Municipal Accounting Unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, with the help of the Municipal Budget Officer, to institutionalize the DRRM Office, create the required items for the required three (3) staff under the LDRRM Office, in compliance with RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 to undertake the setting of direction, development, and planning on the implementation and coordination of disaster risk management program.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor will create the required permanent items for the DRRM Office with the following permanent items for personnel: 1) MDRRM O – SG 22; 2) SG 18; 3) Clerk II SG 4; 4) LDRRM Assistant SG 8. Positions were already created and filled in.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the department heads concerned to ensure timely implementation of programs, projects and activities (PPAs) funded by NGAs and to supervise the completion thereof. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer determine the availability of cash of all fund transfers which are reported in the books and issue the check in favor of the Source Agencies to return all unutilized/unused amount. In case the source agency can no longer be identified, the check shall be made in favor of the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor will send a memorandum to the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized funds to DOLE. The Municipal Accountant prepared the vouchers for the return of excess funds to DOLE and the unutilized amount was returned to the Source Agency as reflected in the Fund Utilization Report. The funds were returned to DOLE as reflected in the vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately settle the outstanding cash advances through submission of utilization report and other supporting documents. Otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against the accountable officer for any unjustifiable reason due to failure to liquidate cash advances within the prescribed period in compliance with Section 89 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and Civil Service Commission Memorandum Circular No. 12 series of 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer partially settled the outstanding cash advances. The Municipal Mayor will send a memorandum to the Municipal Treasurer to immediately settle the outstanding cash advances. The Municipal Treasurer has partially implemented and still to settle outstanding cash advances amounting to ₱70,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Municipal Mayor to: a) Require all accountable officials and employees of the LGU with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle their cash advances by submitting the necessary documents as evidence of the disbursement made or thru cash refund of the unused cash advances; b) Cause the withholding of salaries and other benefits of employees to settle their unliquidated cash advances if the employees remain adamant, as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002; and c) Exert efforts to locate the whereabouts of those who have retires or out of service, and also issue demand letters to liquidate their outstanding cash advance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters and the Municipal Accountant ensures that the Accountable Officer has no outstanding cash advance if last year FY 2024 there is no remaining unliquidated cash advances in the book that was granted during the year. The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters to each Accountable Officer requesting them to submit Liquidation or to refund the outstanding advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following courses of action: a) The Municipal Engineering Office: • Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee with the complete description and cost segregation of road component for road projects; as well as the report on the repair/maintenance of each local road network; b) The Inventory Committee: • Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN); and • Maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each road and road components; c) The Municipal Accountant • Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office provided the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Team the complete description and cost segregation of road components and the report on the repair/maintenance of each local road network. The Inventory Committee conducted the RPCLRN and maintained the Local Road Network Property Cards. The Audit Team to validate the RPCLRN.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking documents for the liquidation of Emergency Shelter Assistance and to determine the availability of cash of all fund transfers which are reported in the books; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to return all unutilized/unused amount to Source Agencies; and c) Take action of the process and procedures on the write-off of the related Dormant Unliquidated Fund Transfers as provided in Item 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer provided some liquidation documents and returned the unutilized fund to Source Agency as reflected in the vouchers. Municipal Accountant to return unutilized funds. Process for write-off of Dormant Unliquidated Fund Transfers is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all parcels of land owned by the Municipality be registered under the name of the Municipality for its protection from third party claims. To facilitate the titling of the lots, we further recommend that funds be allocated for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Assessor’s Office will be assigned for the registration processing and funds for the Land Title Processing and Land Survey Fee were funded in the Municipal Budget the amount of P1,000,000.00 for the Land Title Processing and Land Survey Fee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: a) Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to conduct an inventory of the Biological Assets, prepare a report of the said assets showing the fair value of each specie and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for proper reconciliation; and b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger for biological assets, update the same based on the report of the Municipal Agriculturist and ensure that proper accounting and recognition is made to Financial Statements as well as to the Notes to the Financial Statements. Moreover, strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under IPSAS 27 in the recognition, measurement, and disclosure of Biological Assets.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist and the Inventory Committee has scheduled the conduct of Biological Inventory on October 2025 and will submit it to the Accounting Office for proper recording. The Inventory Committee has scheduled the conduct of Biological Inventory in October 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer to make a reconciliation of the balances of the Payables Account before paying the same to avoid over/underpayment of the obligations, henceforth establish with certainty the obligations to provide reliable information in the financial statements as provided in Item no. 29 of PPSAS 1, Volume 1 of PPSAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation:

action:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and verify the official
receipts submitted by the RCCs to accurately report the collections
as they occur;

b) Require the RCCs to present the AFs mentioned in Table 4 or present
any proof of remittance so that proper adjustments could be made; and

c) Effect the adjustments on the TF books as a result of the
reconciliation made between the Accounting and Treasurers records.

2.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to verify and validate the dormant accounts from any
available source documents and draw the necessary adjusting entry if it
was a result of an accounting error, or if warranted, include the accounts
in the request for write-off in the following year, in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

3.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds
and Due to Other Funds and effect adjustments to reflect the correct
account balances and fair presentation of the assets and liabilities in the
financial statements, pursuant to Sections 111 and 112 of Presidential
Decree No. 1445.

4.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards to monitor issuances of the
inventories.

Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the
Municipal Treasurer to:

c) Maintain stock cards, and direct the Supply Officer to submit the
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), together with the
Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and other pertinent supporting
documents, to the Accounting Office for recognition of expense of
issued/distributed inventories; and

d) Conduct periodic physical count of the inventory items, prepare and
submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant to come up with
an accurate recording and determination of the Inventories account
balance.

5.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive take the following
courses of action:

a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary
adjustments to correct the misstatements found in Advances to
Contractors and other related accounts;

b) Exert extra efforts to locate the pertinent documents and establish
the correctness and accuracy of the long outstanding balances, and
issue demand letters requiring them to return the unrecouped
advances; and

c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to initiate request for write-off, if
necessary, on the unrecouped balance of ₱3,080.34 which had been
outstanding for more than 10 years.

6.6. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to take the following
courses of action:

a) Enjoin the Inventory Committee to include all PPE items in the
physical count and require the Municipal Treasurer/GSO that
property records, including the RPCPPE, be updated based on the
result thereof, and reconcile the RPCPPE with accounting records to
come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts; and

b) Reconstitute the Appraisal and Disposal Committee and direct the
Municipal Treasurer/GSO to initiate the process of disposal of
unserviceable properties and submit the pertinent documents to the
Accounting Office as basis to derecognize the mentioned
unserviceable properties from the books of accounts, pursuant to
existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

8.12. We recommended for the Municipal Mayor the following courses of
action:
a) Submit the incidence report of malnutrition and incidence of hunger
to justify the implementation of the program;
b) Require the MSWDO to submit a Supplemental Feeding Plan with
budgetary requirements for each activity, the delivery schedule and
the items to be delivered on a particular date shall be known to the
supplier and the DCWs; and
c) Document the actual delivery and transfer to each DCC.
Henceforth, if the program is continued: (i) Stop the delivery during
Saturdays or non-working day; (ii) Provide the necessary transportation
expenses and incidental expenses and condiments; and, (iii) Calculate the
quantity of food items to be purchased on factual basis.

9.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive take the following
courses of action:

a) Stop charging ineligible expenditure items from the 20%
Development Fund and ensure that the development projects be
optimally utilized for the attainment of desirable socio-economic
targets and outcomes of the LGU;

b) Fund for PPAs which are not procurement-and-implementation-
ready be realigned to other priority PPAs which could be readily
procured or implemented;

c) Enjoin the Local Finance Committee to reevaluate the current PPAs,
more particularly on the corresponding expenditure items funded
under the 20% Development Fund, and recommend reversion and
transfer of ineligible PPAs, if possible, to the appropriate funds; and

d) Direct the MPDC to review all its plans and programs to ensure that
what is planned and programmed is in compliance with the proper
charging of the said fund.

10.10. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive the following courses of
action:

a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation
documents immediately to the Municipal Accounting Unit and COA
on the cash advances granted to her so that proper adjustments of
her accountability on the books of accounts can be effected; and

b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters
periodically to enforce the immediate liquidation/settlement of past
due cash advances of the accountable officer concerned and enforce
strict compliance with the rules and regulations on cash advances.

11.19. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive take the following
courses of action:

a) Request from the SB members to ratify a well-defined policy
guideline on the grant of assistance to come up with a resolution in
consonance with the guidelines issued by the DSWD;
b) Municipal Accountant to ascertain the completeness of the required
supporting documents based on the guidelines; and
c) Direct the MSWDO to prepare and submit MATRIX for the AICS
program based on DSWD guidelines as an aid to the SB in the
promulgation of well-defined and specific guidelines that would be
consistently applied in the grant of financial assistance. Likewise,
require her to maintain objectivity, in determining the type, kind, and
amount of financial assistance to be recommended for approval of the
appropriate authority.

12.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a) Enforce the immediate liquidation and/or refund of the unliquidated
cash advances and to refrain from granting additional cash advance
to officials and employees with outstanding balances until the
previous cash advances are fully settled /liquidated;

b) Designate a staff to closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding
cash advances; and

c) Send demand letters to the employees concerned and require the
submission of liquidation documents or refund the amount,
whichever is applicable in the circumstances.

13.5. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure that the
LDRRMF is utilized solely for disaster risk reduction and management
activities.
Moreover, the LCE should require:

a) The LDRRMO to prepare and submit monthly the Report on Sources
and Utilization of LDRRMF, certified correct by the Municipal
Accountant, and submit the same within the reglementary period in
the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002; and

b) The Municipal Accountant to revert the amount reflected in the
Trust Liability-DRRMF account, in the amount of ₱304,104.56, to the
General Fund since the 5-year validity period of the unexpended
balance from CY 2017 has already lapsed.

14.11. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the MENRO
the following courses of action:

a) Strictly prohibit the practice of open burning of solid waste and
establishment/operation of an open dump site within the
municipality, and impose the corresponding sanctions/penalties in
case of violation thereto; and

b) Enjoin all barangay Solid Waste Management (SWM) Committees:
(i) to establish their MRF in a barangay-owned or leased land or any
suitable open space to be determined by the barangay through its
Sanggunian; and, (ii) design the MRFs in such a way that it can
receive, sort, process and store compostable and recyclable material
efficiently and in an environmentally sound manner.

15.8. We recommended that the Municipal Local School Board:

a) Review and approve the SEF Annual Budget for the ensuing year not
later than October 31 of the current year; and

b) Implement the planned and approved PPAs during the year and
utilize the funds fully in order to achieve the purpose for which the
fund was established.

We recommend for the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:
a) Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual physical Inventory-Count; (2) Lack of proper maintenance of records and monitoring of movements of the PPEs; (3) Continued inaction in the disposal of unserviceable properties, contrary to the provisions of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 17, and COA Circular No. 2020-006.
b) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to maintain Property Cards and the necessary Property Ledger Cards and to update both property records based on the procedural guidelines provided in the same Circular to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the LGU’s PPE;
c) Assign a full time General Services Officer or a Property Officer who will competently undertake the management and safekeeping of the LGUs properties; and
d) Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the process of the disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU following the procedural steps cited in such undertaking.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters periodically to enforce
the immediate liquidation/settlement of past due cash advances of all
concerned accountable officers and personnel and enforce strict
compliance with the rules and regulations on cash advances. Require
the active personnel to submit their liquidation documents or refund
the money whichever is applicable in the circumstances.

Further, require an Accountant’s certification that the previous cash
advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books as part
of the supporting documents. Likewise refrain from granting cash
advances from the national government offices or officers not
connected with the municipality in order to safeguard the government
funds from possible losses or misuse.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Inventory Committee to prepare a methodical and
well-planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a
year-round basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the
Municipality have been accounted for and prepare the
corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE), thereon;
b) Direct the Property and Accounting Unit to undertake collaborative
procedure to ensure that all PPEs included in the RCPPE are duly
recorded in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs)
maintained by the Property Unit and the PPELCs maintained by the
Accounting Unit are reconciled;
c) Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the process of the
disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU;
d) Prepare the necessary adjusting entry by drawing a journal entry
voucher to come up with an accurate and reliable value of the PPE
items in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to
include all benefits and remuneration received by the Municipal
officials and employees in the computation of their respective taxable
income in accordance with RMC No. 23-2012 and Section 6 (B) of
Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018, and withhold the correct amount of
tax and remit the same on time to avoid the possibility of incurring
penalties in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal
Accountant and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer being the
Liquidating Officer to require the accounting and remittance of the
collections had by the collectors by issuing demand letters and setting
deadline for them to comply; b) Liquidating Officer to religiously
prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF)
and verify periodically to control and monitor the issuance of
accountable forms to the collectors and only one booklet at a time and
reporting of issued accountable forms be required before a new one is
released.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC and the
Municipal Engineer to ensure that the development projects be
optimally utilized for the attainment of desirable socio-economic
targets and outcomes of the LGU.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer, MPDC and all
other offices concerned, to implement the PPAs within the scheduled
date of implementation as indicated in the Annual Investment Plan and
without further delay to achieve the benefits that can be derived from
the fund.

Moreover, we recommended that the funds for PPAs which are not
readily implementable be realigned to other priority PPAs which are
beneficial to the constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a) The LDRRM Officer to optimally utilize the 70% Mitigation Fund
of the LDRRM Fund and fully implement the PPAs as provided in
the LDRRMFIP;
b) The Municipal Accountant to ensure that the charges made on the
LDRRM Fund complies with the purposes for which the fund had
been established and stop the charges of ineligible expenses to the
said fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive with the help of the
Municipal Budget Officer to institutionalize the DRRM Office, create
items for the required three (3) staff under the LDRRM Office in
compliance with RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 to undertake the setting of
direction, development, and planning on the implementation and
coordination of disaster risk management program.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
1) Direct the Municipal Engineering office to monitor the projects not
only upon completion but at the start, a representative should look
into the compliance of the contractor to the signboard postings; and
2) Likewise, a report required in COA Circular 2013-004 be complied
with and submitted on the prescribed period so that proper offices
could validate its compliance in order to achieve the purpose of
providing full disclosure to the public on the information of public
concern.

We recommended to the LCE to constitute the GFPS Exe Com.
pursuant to Item 4.1 an of RA 9710 to complement the existing TWG.
Also, a Secretariat should also be created to attend to all paper works
relating to the formulation of the GPB and other related reports.

Likewise, we recommended that the GFPS once constituted should
strictly observe the timelines in the preparation, submission of the GPB
and Accomplishment Reports to DILG and other oversight agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the PESO
Manager and Municipal Accountant to ensure that all release of
assistance is supported with complete documentation and adhere to the
guidelines provided for its implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer monitor the
implementation of the projects which have not yet been completed and
ensure that these are satisfactorily accomplished on time.

Also, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
Chairperson should strictly enforce compliance to the 2016 RIRR of
RA 9184 in the conduct of procurement to ensure the implementation of
infrastructure projects particularly on contract completion.
Likewise, impose the corresponding liquidated damages which shall be
deducted from the claims of contractors, if applicable.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer closely monitor the
simultaneous implementation of several projects awarded to the same
contractor to avoid unjustified delays and impose liquidated damages,
if applicable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive review the
expenditures made under the Covid-19 Fund and assess whether these
expenses are for public purpose which would directly support the
objective of the agency in the prevention, mitigation or rehabilitation
from the adverse effects of the Covid-19 virus. Moreover, strictly
comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
department heads concerned to ensure timely implementation of
programs, projects and activities (PPAs) funded by NGAs and to
supervise the completion thereof.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer determine the
availability of cash of all fund transfers which are reported in the books
and issue the check in favor of the Source Agencies to return all
unutilized/unused amount. In case the source agency can no longer be
identified, the check shall be made in favor of the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a) Transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2020 amounting to ₱11,446,485.73 to the Special Trust Fund; b) The amount of Ᵽ38,879.15 for FY 2015 not fully utilized after five years be reverted back to the General Fund; c) Prepare the necessary adjusting entry so that the Trust Liability-DRRM and Government Equity account are fairly presented in the financial statements; and d) Henceforth, strictly observe Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjusting entries in the books of account by reclassifying the erroneous recognition of Supplies Inventory and Property, Plant and Equipment as set forth in the IPSAS 12 and 17- Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment, respectively, so that the affected accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 2

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately settle the outstanding cash advances through submission of utilization report and other supporting documents. Otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against the accountable officer for any unjustifiable reason due to failure to liquidate cash advances within the prescribed period in compliance with Section 89 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and Civil Service Commission Memorandum Circular No. 12 series of 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Local Chief Executive direct the designated Supply Officer to conduct physical inventory, prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGU; b. Strictly comply with Sections 119, 121 and 123 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I on records to be maintained and proper documentation in the issuances of supplies using the prescribed forms.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Require the Supply Officer and the Disposal Committee to prepare the corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the identified unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposal pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445 and in accordance with the rules and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 92-386; and b. After the disposal, require the Supply Officer to submit the necessary documents, together with the official receipt to the Accounting Office, as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The LGU is still undergoing the completion of the process of disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all Biological Assets be inventoried and properly accounted for and that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledger for biological assets and ensure proper recognition in the Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS 27.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 6

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) require the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to submit monthly the Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project/Activity to the audit team; and b) that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for the BGCM account for easy monitoring/tracking of its utilization.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 7

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC and Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements as provided in Annex A of this AOM, in compliance to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020, respectively.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 8

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MSWDO/MDRRMO to provide the accounting unit copies of the RIS/distribution list of relief goods. Further, require the GSO to ensure the submission of the Monthly Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Utilization of Relief Goods.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 9

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) MSWDO cause the immediate submission of the required documents as prescribed under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04 to ensure that the beneficiaries comply; and b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the liquidation documents are complete and sufficient. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the required warranty securities. Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final acceptance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 11

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Direct the Supply Officer to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection of all its government vehicles subjected to repairs. 2. Require that the Supply Officer prepare the Certificate of Acceptance and Report of Waste Material and approved by the LCE. 3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a ledger card for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU to monitor whether the property is economically repairable.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Finding No. 12

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: 1. Require all accountable officials and employees of the LGU with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle their cash advances by submitting the necessary documents as evidence of the disbursement made or thru cash refund of the unused cash advances; 2. Cause the AAR to withhold salaries and other benefits of employees to settle their unliquidated cash advances if the employees remain adamant as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002; 3. Exert efforts to locate the whereabouts of those who have retired or out of service, and also issue demand letters to liquidate their outstanding cash advance; 4. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop granting additional cash advance to employees with unliquidated cash advances and issue certification that employee has no existing cash advance before grant of the same; and 5. Send demand letters to the employees concerned and forward the same to COA for the issuance of final demand, and initiate filing of cases pursuant to Section 128 of PD 1445, for restitution of funds by the erring officials.
observations.managementAction: AAR Noted (various AAR years cited) ; AAR on actions include arrangements to monetize leave credits and deductions from payroll where applicable
observations.reason: Persons liable received demand letters but despite effort made by management, there is still no action from the accountable officers. The management also made an arrangement with the LGU to deduct in payroll the outstanding cash advance and not all employees had agreed/refunded yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to: 1) Take action on the process and procedures on the write-off of the related receivable accounts; and 2) Provide Allowance for Impairment for the uncollectible accounts pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to reduce the affected receivable accounts to its appropriate carrying amounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Finding No. 4; AAR 2011 Finding No. 11; AAR 2016-005 cited
observations.reason: The management will identify those receivable accounts which are non-moving and will request for write-off once the documents supporting the request for write-off are retrieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. Conduct the physical inventory of all PPEs. Prescribed composition of the committee for the conduct of physical inventory is provided in Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992. b. Reconcile the balances recorded in the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) of the Accounting Office with the balances reported in the Property Cards (PC) maintained by the Property Officer/GSO. c. Require all heads of the department to prepare a list of all existing properties under their respective offices and submit the same to the inventory committee to facilitate the inventory taking.
observations.managementAction: AAR (various years cited: 2016,2015,2014,2013,2012 references)

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Engineering Office: • Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee with the report on the repair/maintenance of each local road network; The Inventory Committee: • Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN); • Maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each road and road components; The Municipal Accountant: • Adhere to the accounting policy on non-inclusion of residual value in the computation of the depreciation expense for the depreciable component of the Local Road Network; • Review the lapsing schedule and recompute the completed road network based on the correct useful life. • Prepare the adjusting entry to correct the overstatement of Depreciation Expense to rectify the error committed; • Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Finding No. 6; prior AAR references

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: transfer and record the unexpended balance for CY 2019 to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund book. Moreover, the unutilized/unexpended balance of ₱928,806.89 for CY 2013 be reverted back to General Fund as required in Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Finding No. 7; AAR 2017 Finding No. 8; AAR 2016 Finding No. 9; AAR 2015 Finding No. 7

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and all others concerned to prioritize the implementation of development projects in order to ensure that the 20% DF is optimally utilized.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Finding No. 12; AAR 2018 Finding No. 9; AAR 2017 Finding No. 10; AAR 2016 Finding No. 13; AAR 2014 Finding No. 9; AAR 2013 Finding No. 4; AAR 2012 Finding No. 7
observations.reason: The 20% projects reported that prior year projects were already implemented; some have reached 86% completion as discussed in Part II.

observations.recommendation: To correct the deficiencies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the HRMO: 1. Determine the necessity, assess the duties and functions, taking into account their expertise, experience and capacity to handle the position in order to come up with the ideal number of personnel to be hired; 2. Indicate in the contract of service the designation of the JO worker and the specific office/department to which he/she belongs; 3. Stipulate the pay of job order workers on a daily basis as stated in CSC Resolution No. 0200790 dated June 5, 2002.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Finding No. 15; AAR 2017 Finding No. 12
observations.reason: The management indicated contracts for Job Orders were already submitted and efforts made to specify pay on daily basis.

observations.recommendation: In order to avoid the above consequences, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Persons Liable/Responsible to immediately settle their unsettled disallowances or require the Municipal Accountant to withhold any amount due them until the disallowance is fully settled.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Finding No. 16
observations.reason: Persons liable settled the disallowances thru voluntary payment to MTO but process was interrupted due to Supreme Court en banc opinion partially granting petition that changed refund requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to: a) Enjoin the department heads to strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on prioritization, training of personnel responsible for planning and implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that intended beneficiaries are assured of the services; and b) Require the Sangguniang Bayan to formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinance with the assistance of the GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Finding No. 17; AAR 2017 Finding No. 11
observations.reason: The Sanggunian formulated and passed a local GAD code/ordinance with assistance; GFPS efforts ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the inventories that were issued/expensed; (b) Strictly comply with Sections 123 of Presidential Decree and Section C.3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodian, and hence, the inventory and government equity account be fairly stated.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all parcels of land owned by the Municipality be registered under the name of the Municipality for its protection from third party claims; to facilitate the titling of the lots, we further recommend that funds be allocated for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Finding No. 3
observations.reason: Out of 10 parcels, 2 are already with Title; 8 are still in process of titling. The LGU is gathering documents and waiting for titles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-Agency Accounts, “Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds” to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Finding No. 4
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going and necessary items are being prepared; some reconciling items remain unidentified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable officers to strictly comply with the regulations on the reporting and remittance of collections and the Municipal Treasurer/Cashier to update cashbooks and conduct periodic reconciliation with accounting records. Any difference should be immediately reconciled and corresponding adjustment made to affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 5; AAR 2014 Finding No. 3; AAR 2012 Finding No. 2
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer is updating the cashbook and reconciliation of records between Treasurer and Accounting Office is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the concerned affected officials and employees to refund the amounts received for unauthorized allowances and refrain from granting similar allowances, incentives and benefits without legal basis; strictly observe the limitation for appropriation and expenditures for personal services as provided under GAA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Finding No. 2
observations.reason: The LGU received an En Banc opinion from Supreme Court granting petition that affected the requirement to refund some disallowed amounts; some persons remained liable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adopt stricter procedures in allowing only mandatory deductions from salaries and ensure net take home pay will not be reduced below Four Thousand Pesos (P4,000.00) as required by GAA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Finding No. 10
observations.reason: The LGU made efforts to ensure required net take home pay per month required by GAA; employees with existing loans continue to have mandatory deductions as allowed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2020
amounting to ₱11,446,485.73 to the Special Trust Fund;
b) The amount of Ᵽ38,879.15 for FY 2015 not fully utilized after five
years be reverted back to the General Fund;
c) Prepare the necessary adjusting entry so that the Trust Liability-
DRRM and Government Equity account are fairly presented in the
financial statements; and
d) Henceforth, strictly observe Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA
Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary
adjusting entries in the books of accounts by reclassifying the erroneous
recognition of Supplies Inventory and Property, Plant and Equipment as
set forth in the IPSAS 12 and 17- Inventories and Property, Plant and
Equipment, respectively, so that the affected accounts are fairly
presented in the financial statements.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to immediately settle the outstanding cash advances
through submission of utilization report and other supporting
documents. Otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against the
accountable officer for any unjustifiable reason due to failure to
liquidate cash advances within the prescribed period in compliance with
Section 89 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and Civil Service
Commission Memorandum Circular No. 12 series of 2012.

We recommended that the:
a. Local Chief Executive direct the designated Supply Officer to
conduct physical inventory, prepare and submit the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and Report of Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI), pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS
Manual for LGU;
b. Strictly comply with Sections 119, 121 and 123 of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs, Volume I on records to be maintained and
proper documentation in the issuances of supplies using the prescribed
forms.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Supply Officer and the Disposal Committee to prepare
the corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the identified unserviceable
properties and initiate the process of disposal pursuant to Section
79 of PD 1445 and in accordance with the rules and procedures
provided in COA Circular No. 92-386; and
b. After the disposal, require the Supply Officer to submit the
necessary documents, together with the official receipt to the
Accounting Office, as the basis of the latter to drop the
unserviceable properties from the books of accounts in accordance
with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to conduct an inventory of the
Biological Assets, prepare a report of the said assets showing the
fair value of each specie and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant for proper reconciliation; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger
for biological assets, update the same based on the report of the
Municipal Agriculturist and ensure that proper accounting and
recognition is made to Financial Statements as well as to the Notes to
the Financial Statements. Moreover, strictly adhere to the guidelines
set forth under IPSAS 27 in the recognition, measurement, and
disclosure of Biological Assets.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance
Committee (LFC) to submit monthly the Report on Fund
Utilization and Status of Program/Project/Activity to the audit team. We
also recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate
Subsidiary Ledger for the BGCM account for easy monitoring/tracking
of its utilization.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC and
Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements as
provided in Annex A of this AOM, in compliance to GPPB Circular
No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 dated
September 16, 2020, respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
MSWDO/MDRRMO to provide the accounting unit copies of the
RIS/distribution list of relief goods. Further, require the GSO to ensure
the submission of the Monthly Report on the Receipt and
Distribution/Utilization of Relief Goods.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a) MSWDO to cause the immediate submission of the required
documents as prescribed under the rules and procedures of DSWD
Memorandum Circular No. 04 to ensure that the beneficiary of the
social amelioration provisions comply with the documentary
requirements and guarantee attainment of the primary purpose of
the social protection program; and
b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the liquidation documents are
complete and sufficient.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to require the contractors concerned to post the subject
warranty securities. This recommendation is without prejudice to the
issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the
contractors to post the required warranty securities.
Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in the implementation
of infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty
securities before final acceptance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Supply Officer to conduct pre-repair
evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its
government vehicles subjected to repairs.
2. Require the Supply Officer to prepare the Certificate of Acceptance
and Report of Waste Material and approved by the LCE.
3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a ledger card for the
expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every
vehicle owned by the LGU to monitor whether the property is
economically repairable.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action: a.) To direct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to payees with unrecorded collections and to cancel stale checks in accordance to Section 83 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual; and b.) To direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to record deposited collections, debit memorandum and restoration of stale checks which were not yet taken up in the books in CY 2019 in accordance to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary adjusting memorandum.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that for the Municipal Mayor to: 1. Require all accountable officers and employees of the LGU with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle their cash advances by submitting the necessary documents as evidence of the disbursement made or thru cash refund of the unused cash advances; 2. Cause the officers withholding of salaries and other benefits of employees to settle their unliquidated cash advances if the employees remains adamant as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002; 3. Exert efforts to locate the whereabouts of those who have retired or out of service, and also issue demand letters to liquidate their outstanding cash advance; 4.
