Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop granting additional cash advance to employees with unliquidated cash advances and issue certification that employee has no existing cash advance before grant of the same; and 5. Send demand letters to the employees concerned and forward the same to COA for the issuance of final demand, and initiate filing of cases pursuant to Section 128 of PD 1445, for restitution of funds by the erring officials.
observations.managementAction: AAR sent demand letters thru postal office with return cards to make sure that the outstanding cash advances to officers and employees are identified despite receipt of demand letters, there is no action from the accountable officers. The AAR management also made an effort with the accountable officers still in the LGU to identify the accountable officers. The AAR management also made an arrangement with the accountable officers still in the LGU to deduct the outstanding cash advances in their payroll. The AAR management will request the accountable officers to refund the amounts received for unauthorized allowances. The AAR management recommended to the LGU to deduct the outstanding cash advances in their payroll.
observations.reason: There were demand letters sent. Despite the effort made by management, there is still no action from the accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the disallowances which had become final and executor having been issued with NFD as Receivables. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for each official/employee determined liable/responsible for the amount disallowed for monitoring of the liability and settlement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant maintained SL of each employee. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for each official/employee determined liable/responsible for the amount disallowed for monitoring of the liability and settlement.
observations.reason: There were disallowances with NFD issued and subsidiary ledgers maintained; however full monitoring and settlement processes remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1) Take action on the process and procedures on the write-off of the related receivable accounts; and 2) Provide for Allowance for Impairment once the documents had been retrieved and once the accounts had been determined uncollectible, prepare the necessary write-off documents pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to reduce the affected receivable accounts to its appropriate carrying amounts.
observations.managementAction: The management indicated that they will identify those receivable accounts that appeared in the financial statements. Lack of supporting documents to support the request for write-off. The management will identify the accounts in the FS and request the necessary documents for write-off.
observations.reason: Dormant receivable accounts still exist; lack of supporting documents to support write-off. Management will identify accounts and retrieve documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Conduct the physical inventory of all PPEs. Prescribed committee for the conduct of physical inventory is provided in Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992. While prescribed forms to be used in the reporting is provided in the Manual for NGAS. b. Reconcile the balances recorded in the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) of the Accounting Office with the balances reported in the Property Cards (PC) maintained by the Property Officer/GSO. c. Require all heads of the department to prepare a list of all existing properties under their respective offices and submit the same to the inventory committee to facilitate the inventory taking.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will conduct physical balances of PPEs. Prescribed committee for the conduct of physical inventory is provided in Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. The Inventory Committee will prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN); maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each roads and road components; The Municipal Accountant will prepare necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: There were efforts to conduct inventories and reconcile, but PPE Ledger Card and Property Card were unreconciled; physical inventory work ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office: • Provide the necessary adjustments. The Inventory Committee: • Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN); • Maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each roads and road components; The Municipal Accountant: • Adhere to the accounting policy on non-inclusion of residual value in the computation of the depreciation expense for the depreciable component of the Local Road Network; • Review the lapsing schedule and recompute the completed road network based on the correct useful life. • Prepare the adjusting entry to correct the overstatement of Depreciation Expense to rectify the error committed; • Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer will make necessary adjustments. The Inventory Team conducted physical count of inventories. The Local Road Network Inventory Committee is still undergoing physical count of Local Road Network which belongs to the Municipal Map and survey of local roads is still on-going. The Municipal Accountant will make the necessary adjusting entry and observe the established rules provided in IPSAS 17-Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and COA Circular No. 2003-007.
observations.reason: The Road Network inventory work is ongoing; LGU not fully compliant with COA Circular 2015-008 and computation adjustments remain to be completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to transfer and record the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – DRRM in the Trust Fund books. Moreover, the unutilized/unexpended balance of ₱928,806.89 for CY 2013 be reverted back to General Fund as required in Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant transferred the unexpended QRF and MOOE to STF after 5 years was reverted back to GF. The AAR reported the unexpended balance in the STF after year-end.
observations.reason: Related discussion in Part II of this report indicates QRF amounts were transferred, however some unexpended balances in prior years were not reverted back to General Fund and some transfers for CY 2020 were not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct balances of the Trust Liabilities and Due to NGAs account and to strictly adhere the prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording of the transactions of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjusting entry was made to reflect the correct balances. The Municipal Accountant complied and made the adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to take up the receipts from PCA of ₱236,000.00 in the General Fund Books to reflect the correct balances of the income and payable account and to strictly adhere to the prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording of transactions of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjustment for the receipt of PCA shares was made. The Municipal Accountant complied and made the adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the computation of the depreciation and observe the established rules provided in IPSAS 17-Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and COA Circular No. 2003-007. We also recommended that the necessary adjusting entry be prepared to rectify the error committed.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjustment was made to correct the misstatement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Treasury the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to strictly monitor and carefully evaluate the implementation of the PPAs for which the fund transfers were intended for; direct the Municipal Engineer to administer, supervise and control the implementation of the PPAs related to engineering and public works and establish close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator; direct the concerned Department Heads to have close coordination with Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer regarding the PPAs related to their respective offices; direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking documents for the liquidation of Emergency Shelter Assistance and to determine the availability of cash of all fund transfers which are reported in the books; direct the Municipal Accountant to return all unutilized/unused amount to Source Agencies; and take action on the process and procedures on the write-off of the related Dormant Unliquidated Fund Transfers as provided in Item 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The AAR recommended various actions. The Municipal Treasurer returned some unutilized funds to the SDO of Beneficiaries, the AAR completed some processes. The Municipal Accountant returned amounts for certain items. The Municipal Engineer will administer and supervise projects.
observations.reason: Some amounts were returned and some projects completed; however, unliquidated advances remained outstanding and full compliance with returns/write-offs not completed.

observations.recommendation: To correct the deficiencies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and all others concerned to prioritize the implementation of development projects in order to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer reported that prior year infrastructure projects were not implemented due to COVID-19 pandemic travel restriction. The AAR noted that the projects were not implemented at end of year. The LGU is to prioritize development projects.
observations.reason: Projects were not implemented due to COVID-19 pandemic travel restriction; some activities deferred.

observations.recommendation: 1. To utilize the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Funds that shall address the four thematic areas of Disaster Prevention, Disaster Mitigation, Disaster Preparedness, Disaster Response and Disaster Rehabilitation Recovery. Please refer to the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Memorandum No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 for the allowable projects and activities. 2. To instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to charge to the Fund only disaster related expenditures based on the Approved Plan. While the maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRM Office should be charged to the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The AAR noted the LGU had not fully utilized the LDRRMF. The LGU received ordinance realigning funds for COVID-19 and did procure certain items. Some amounts remained unutilized.
observations.reason: A portion of LDRRMF was utilized; remaining unutilized balances remained due to pandemic-related reallocation and delays; procurement carried out but not all funds obligated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Solid Waste Management Board to identify solid waste management problems and issues within the area and to propose appropriate projects, programs and activities that would enhance proper solid waste management of the locality. Further, require them to be able to implement programs and activities that would enhance proper solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is waiting for the approval of the Solid Waste Management Plan. The Sanggunian formulated and passed a local GAD code and assistance of GFPS to support activities. AAR noted that the LGU is now able to implement activities and waiting for approval.
observations.reason: The Solid Waste Management Plan is done and awaiting approval; implementation pending approval.

observations.recommendation: To correct the deficiencies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the HRMO: 1. To determine the necessity, assess the duties and functions, taking into account their expertise, experience and capacity to handle the position in order to come up with the ideal number of personnel to be hired; 2. To indicate in the contract of service the designation of the JO worker and the specific office/department to which he/she belongs; and 3. To stipulate the pay of job order workers on a daily basis as stated in CSC Resolution No. 0200790 dated June 5, 2002.
observations.managementAction: The designated Disbursing Officer stamped 'PAID' on the payrolls, DVs and supporting documents. The Local Government made efforts to determine the necessity of job orders and designate positions with specific offices. Contracts for Job Orders were already submitted.
observations.reason: Efforts made; some job orders already arranged and contracts submitted, but full reassessment and conversion to daily rate not complete for all JO workers.

observations.recommendation: In order to avoid the above consequences, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Persons Liable/Responsible to immediately settle their disallowances or require the Municipal Accountant to withhold any amount due them until the appeal of disallowance is fully settled.
observations.managementAction: Persons liable settled the disallowances thru voluntary payment but it was stopped due to the receipt of the En Banc opinion from Supreme Court partially granting the appeal. LGU submitted certifications and partial settlements were made by some persons liable.
observations.reason: Partial settlements made; some cases pending final Supreme Court decision; full settlement awaiting court outcome.

observations.recommendation: a. Enjoin the department heads to strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on prioritization, localization and training of personnel responsible for planning and implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided; and b. Require the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinance with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta for Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.
observations.managementAction: The Sanggunian was not able to attend training due to COVID-19 pandemic. The LGU received assistance from GFPS and the Sanggunian considered formulation of a local GAD code. Some initiatives were made to mainstream GAD activities.
observations.reason: Training and full localization delayed due to pandemic; some steps taken to mainstream GAD but full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (a) Local Chief Executive direct the General Supply Officer to prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), pursuant to Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 12; (b) Strictly comply with Sections 123 of the Manual on Property Custodian on proper documentation in the issuances of supplies using the prescribed forms; and (c) Conduct physical count of inventories semestrally and submit the report.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Team conducted physical count of inventories. The Supply Officer failed to maintain the updated Stock Cards and was not able to prepare and submit Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). The LGU did not conduct the required semestral Physical Count resulting to non-preparation of RPCI as of December 31, 2020. The AAR recommended compliance.
observations.reason: Supply Officer maintained stock cards but they were not updated; SSMI/RPCI not submitted semestrally; physical counts incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all parcels of land owned by the Municipality be registered under the name of the Municipality for its protection from third party claims. To facilitate the titling of the lots, we further recommend that funds be allocated for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Assessor is now gathering documents for the titling of the Land. The LGU is waiting for the Title of the Land. The LGU indicated that funds are needed for the titling process.
observations.reason: Out of 10 parcels of land, 2 are already with Land Title; the remaining 8 are still in the process of titling; LGU awaiting documents and allocating funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-Agency Accounts, 'Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds' to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if appropriate, to bring the affected accounts into agreement.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of records are still on-going. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant are updating the cashbook so that it will be reconciled with the accounting records and the Municipal Treasurer's Cashbook.
observations.reason: Reconciliation work is ongoing; some reconciling items remain unidentified.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or the Designated Disbursing Officer exercise due care in the release of payments for every transaction and to stamp 'PAID' on the payrolls, vouchers and its supporting documents upon release of payment to comply with established rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The designated Disbursing Officer stamped 'PAID' on the payrolls, DVs and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to prepare the duly certified list or Tax Rolls showing all the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and submit the same to the Municipal Treasurer for recording in the AR and in turn submit to the Municipal Accountant a list of Taxpayers for recording the RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts pursuant to Section 248, Chapter 2, Title Two of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted a duly certified list or Tax Rolls to the Municipal Accountant. The certified list was used by the Municipal Accountant to prepare the RPT/SET Receivable.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require elected officials to issue a government official receipt (Accountable Form No. 51) for all collections made so that proper financial accountability could be established. Further, ensure that all future payment to these leagues for membership dues, registration fees and others be receipted with a government official receipt AF No. 51.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted AF No. 51 for the collections. The LGU ensured that future payments will be receipted with a government official receipt.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and all accountable officers to strictly comply with regulations on the reporting and remittance of collections, and the Municipal Treasurer regularly update the cashbooks so that it will be reconciled with the accounting records. Cashbook should be reconciled with the accounting records and discrepancies immediately adjusted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is still updating the cashbook. The cashbook of the Municipal Treasurer is not yet updated. Reconciliation of records are on-going between the accounting records and the Treasurer’s records.
observations.reason: Cashbook updating and reconciliations are ongoing; full reconciliation and adjustments not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refrain from granting similar allowances, incentives and benefits to individuals in the absence of any legal or statutory basis. Likewise, strictly observe the limitation for the appropriation and expenditures for personal services as provided for in Section 325(a) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The LGU received an En Banc opinion from the Supreme Court granting the persons liable not to refund the disallowed amounts. However, copies of the disallowed Official Receipts were not attached. The LGU made effort to settle the matter and the affected persons provided partial settlements where applicable.
observations.reason: En Banc decision partially granted appeal; some persons liable settled amounts; documentation of settlements incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adopt stricter procedures and allow only mandatory required net deductions from salaries accruing to employees so that their monthly net take home pay will not be reduced to an amount lower than Four Thousand Pesos (P4,000.00), as provided in Section 37 of RA 10155 or the General Appropriations Act 2012.
observations.managementAction: The LGU made an effort to help employees achieve the required minimum net take home pay per month as required by GAA, but due to existing loans some employees had below minimum net take home pay per month. The LGU will enforce stricter procedures moving forward.
observations.reason: Some employees still had net take home pay below P4,000 due to existing loans; management taking steps to address the issue.

observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor the following courses of
action:
a.) To direct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to payees
with unclaimed checks at least one month before a check becomes
stale and to cancel stale checks in accordance to Section 83 of the
Local Treasury Operations Manual; and
b.) To direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to
record deposited collections, debit memorandum and restoration of
stale checks which were not yet taken up in the books in CY 2019
in accordance to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
Also, may we be furnished of the journal entry vouchers (JEVs) to
record the adjusting entries for purposes of verification.

2.17 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
1. Require all accountable officials and employees of the LGU with
outstanding cash advances to immediately settle their cash
advances by submitting the necessary documents as evidence of the
disbursement made or thru cash refund of the unused cash
advances;
2. Cause the withholding of salaries and other benefits of employees
to settle their unliquidated cash advances if the employees remains
adamant as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002;
3. Exert efforts to locate the whereabouts of those who have retired or
out of service, and also issue demand letters to liquidate their
outstanding cash advance;
4. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop granting additional cash
advance to employees with unliquidated cash advances and issue
certification that employee has no existing cash advance before
grant of the same; and
5. Send demand letters to the employees concerned and forward the
same to COA for the issuance of final demand, and initiate filing of
cases pursuant to Section 128 of PD 1445, for restitution of funds
by the erring officials.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare a journal entry voucher to record the
disallowances which had become final and executor having been issued
with NFD as Receivables. Also, we recommend that the Municipal
Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for each official/employee
determined liable/responsible for the amount disallowed for monitoring
of the liability and settlement.

4.8 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
1) Take action on the process and procedures on the write-off of the
related receivable accounts; and
2) Provide Allowance for Impairment corresponding to the
uncollectible accounts pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to
reduce the affected receivable accounts to its appropriate carrying
amounts.

5.8 In her letter reply dated July 27, 2020, the Municipal Accountant explained
that Management was not able to conduct physical inventory for CY 2019
due to lack of initiative from the Inventory Team. She also stated in her reply
that they will create a new Inventory Team which duties and responsibilities
includes disposal and appraisal of unserviceable properties so that the LGU
will be able to report accurately its PPE balance, likewise, they will designate
a new Property Officer who will work on the inventory and management of
the LGU properties.

6.7 We recommended the following courses of action:
The Municipal Engineering Office:
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee
with the complete description and cost segregation of road
component for road projects; as well as the report on the
repair/maintenance of each local road network;
The Inventory Committee:
Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Local
Road Network (RPCLRN);
Maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each roads
and road components;
The Municipal Accountant
Adhere to the accounting policy on non-inclusion of residual value in
the computation of the depreciation expense for the depreciable
component of the Local Road Network;
Review the lapsing schedule and recompute the completed road
network based on the correct useful life.
Prepare the adjusting entry to correct the overstatement of
Depreciation Expense to rectify the error committed;
Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for
every road network.

7.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to transfer and record the unexpended balance of the QRF
and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the Trust Fund
liability – DRRM account in the Trust Fund book. Moreover, the
unutilized/unexpended balance of ₱928,806.89 for CY 2013 be reverted
back to General Fund as required in Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA
Circular 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012.

8.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reflect
the correct balances of the Trust Liabilities and Due to NGAs account
and to strictly adhere to the prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts for
Local Government Units provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in
recording of the transactions of the LGU.

9.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to take up
the receipts from PCA of Ᵽ236,000.00 in the General Fund Books to
reflect the correct balances of the income and payable account and to
strictly adhere to the prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts for Local
Government Units provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording
of transactions of the LGU.

10.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to review the computation of the depreciation expense and
observe the established rules provided in IPSAS 17-Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE) and COA Circular No. 2003-007. We also
recommended that the necessary adjusting entry be prepared to rectify
the error committed.

11.21 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor the following courses of
action:
e.) Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to
strictly monitor and carefully evaluate the implementation of the
PPAs for which the fund transfers were intended for;
f.) Direct the Municipal Engineer to administer, supervise and control
the implementation of the PPAs related to engineering and public
works and establish close coordination with the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator;
g.) Direct the concerned Department Heads to have close coordination
with Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and
Municipal Engineer regarding to the PPAs related to their
respective offices; and
h.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking documents
for the liquidation of Emergency Shelter Assistance and to
determine the availability of cash of all fund transfers which are
reported in the books;
i.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to return all unutilized/unused
amount to Source Agencies; and
j.) Take action of the process and procedures on the write-off of the
related Dormant Unliquidated Fund Transfers as provided in Item
8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2015.

12.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and all others
concerned to prioritize the implementation of development projects in
order to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized
pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

13.10 We recommended for the Local Chief Executive:
1. To utilize the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Funds that shall address the four thematic areas of Disaster
Prevention, Disaster Mitigation, Disaster Preparedness, Disaster
Response and Disaster Rehabilitation Recovery. Please refer to the
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Memorandum No. 2013-1
dated March 25, 2013 for the allowable projects and activities.
2. To instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant
to charge to the Fund only disaster related expenditures based on the
Approved Plan. While the maintenance and other operating
expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRM Office should be
charged to the General Fund.

14.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Solid
Waste Management Board to identify solid waste management problems
and issues within the area and propose appropriate projects, programs
and activities that would enhance proper solid waste management of the
municipality.

15.7 To correct the deficiencies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
and the HRMO:
4. To determine the necessity, assess the duties and functions, taking
into account their expertise, experience and capacity to handle the
position in order to come up with the ideal number of personnel to
be hired;
5. To indicate in the contract of service the designation of the JO
worker and the specific office/department to which he/she belongs.
6. To stipulate the pay of job order workers on a daily basis as stated
in CSC Resolution No. 0200790 dated June 5, 2002.

16.5 In order to avoid the above consequences, we recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the Persons Liable/Responsible to immediately
settle their disallowances or require the Municipal Accountant to
withhold any amount due them until the disallowance is fully settled.

17.13 We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Enjoin the department heads to strictly comply with the provisions
of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-
01 on prioritization, and training of personnel responsible for
planning and implementation of GAD programs, projects and
activities to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related
undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the
services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided; and
b. Require the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local GAD code or
ordinance with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the
localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is
mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local
development plans.

We recommend that all parcels of land owned by the Municipality be registered under the name of the Municipality for its protection from third party claims. To facilitate the titling of the lots, we further recommend that funds be allocated for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor is in charge on the registration of the properties of the LGU, however, supporting documents are still lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board require all heads of schools of the municipality to actively participate in the preparation of the budget of SEF. Also, strictly implement the provisions of DepEd, DBM, DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: All school heads will discuss the problem in their respective schools and come up with a plan/programs prioritizing the much needed project and present to the Local School Board for the budget preparation on the said budget.

observations.recommendation: 1.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the Special Education Tax earned to correct the SET Receivable and Deferred Special Education Fund Tax.

2.15 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all accountable officials and employees of the LGU with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle their cash advances by submitting the necessary documents as proof of the disbursement made or thru cash refund of the unused cash advances. Also, exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of those who have retired or out of service, and cause the liquidation of their outstanding cash advance.

3.11 We recommended that the:
(a) Local Chief Executive direct the General Supply Officer to prepare
and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and
Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), pursuant to
Philippines Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 12.
Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare
necessary adjusting entries to record the inventories that were
issued/expensed.
(b) Strictly comply with Sections 123 of Presidential Decree and Section
C.3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodianship on the
proper documentation in the issuances of supplies using the prescribed
forms.

4.6 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the cash in bank and accounts payable balances and to strictly adhere to the prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording of transactions of the LGU.

5.10 We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the taxes withheld on payment for supplies/materials and services of the Municipality. Henceforth, ensure that the taxes withheld on payment for supplies/materials and services are remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue within ten (10) days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding accrued.

6.11 We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the person responsible in the preparation of the payroll to enforce the mandatory deduction of the employee’s share and remit the same including the government share to the respective insurance fund.

7.10 We reiterate from prior years’ recommendation that Management expedite the implementation of the programs/projects in order to serve the purpose for which the funds were released and not to deprived of the benefits that may derived by the constituents. Also, submit copy/ies of the MOAs for each fund received that serve as a guide in the implementation of the projects.

Furthermore, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized balance of completed projects to the source agencies in compliance with Section 4.9 and Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

8.9 We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2018 amounting to ₱2,556,502.42 to the Special Trust Fund, and the amount of ₱982,806.89 for FY 2013 not fully utilized after five (5) years be reverted back to the general fund and be made available for other social services to be identified by the local sanggunian in accordance to Section 21 of RA 10121 and require the Local Budget Officer to appropriate 5% of the total estimated revenue from regular sources for LDRRMF.

9.10 We recommend that the Municipality expedite the completion of the development projects and optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund in order to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as envisioned in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

10.7 We recommend that Municipal Solid Waste Management Board should prioritize the preparation of the amended 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) for approval by the NSWMC and strictly implement the planned ordinances and environmental laws to ensure the protection and general welfare of the constituents.

11.9 We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to refrain from disbursing local funds for activities that are religious in nature pursuant to Sections 335 and 305 of RA No. 7160, and Section 4 (2) of P.D. 1445. Likewise, solicitations on personal capacity and which is not in the exigency of the service, should not be reimbursed nor paid out of the local government funds.

In order to present a reliable balance of the Cash in Bank account at year end, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor / Municipal Accountant verify the reconciling items and to record the adjusting or correcting journal entries. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should be reminded to prepare the reconciliation with the end objective of reporting an accurate balance of the account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant Finding made the necessary adjusting correcting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require all accountable officials and employees of the LGU with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle their cash advances by submitting the necessary documents as proof of the disbursement made or thru cash refund of the unused cash advances. If request for liquidation remained not acted, we recommend for the issuance of demand letter. If it is still not acted by the accountable officer, appropriate legal action shall be filed against the employees. For employees who already have transferred to other government agency, seek assistance from the respective agency head by sending the demand letter thru them. Also, exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of those who have retired or out of service, and cause the liquidation of their outstanding cash advance. This recommendation is without prejudice with the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The LGU AAR issued a Finding demand letters thru portal Related office with discussion is in 2015 return cards, finding no. 2, AAR despite the effort made, finding no. 2 still no action from the AAR accountable officers. Also, the LGU made an arrangement with the AAR accountable officers still connected with the LGU. AAR monetization of leave credits if there is an outstanding cash advances leave credits if there is an outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Though the Finding effort made, still no action from the AAR accountable officers. The LGU made an arrangement with the AAR accountable officers still connected with the LGU. AAR monetization of leave credits if there is an outstanding cash advances. For employees who already have transferred to other government agency, appropriate legal action is pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-Agency Accounts, “Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds” to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if appropriate, to bring the affected accounts into an agreement.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation still on going.
observations.reason: There are still unidentified reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: (a) return the unexpended balance of completed projects to the source agencies in compliance with Section 4.9 and Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; (b) fast track the implementation of the programs/projects in order to serve the purpose for which the funds were released to the Municipality and; (c) submit copy/ies of the MOA for each fund received that serve as a guide in the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: (a) send immediately liquidate the funds received through submission of utilization report; (b) return the unexpended balance of completed projects to the source agencies; (c) fast track the implementation of the projects.
observations.reason: Liquidation reports for the 21 barangays submitted but finding no. 7 for ESA cash advance were submitted by the Mun. Treasurer to the Accounting Office on the day of the exit conference, however, three barangays are still on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2017 amounting to ₱1,873,773.64 to the Special Trust Fund and the total amount of ₱1,302,714.53 for FYs 2011 and 2012 not fully utilized after five (5) years be reverted back to the general fund and be made available for other social services to be identified by the local sanggunian in accordance to Section 21 of RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: AAR take action on the reversion of the unexpended/unutilized balance to the General Fund and Special Trust Fund.
observations.reason: The amount of P1,171,507.03 for FY’s 2011 and 2012 was reverted back on May, 2018. The unexpended balance for CY 2017 was transferred to Special Trust Fund in May, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board prepare the amended 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan compliant with the requirements and provisions set forth in Sections 16 and 23 of RA No. 9003, and implement the plan to ensure efficient disposal of Municipality’s solid wastes.
observations.managementAction: AAR conducted waste analysis characterization study in 2016. Findings in 2016 and 2015. The Municipal MENRO already conducted waste analysis characterization study to all barangays.
observations.reason: As of report date, thirteen (13) barangays submitted the WACS report, while eleven (11) barangays has not yet submitted the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials of the Municipality, to establish the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and allocate one (1) percent of the IRA in the LGUs annual budget in compliance with Republic Act 9344. It is further recommended that LCPC shall propose and implement programs, projects and activities allowed under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR issued an Executive Order for the creation of the LCPC and also, included in the 2019 allocation for the LCPC.
observations.reason: 1% LCPC was not provided in 2018 approved budget. Allocation for the LCPC was included in the 2019 budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management determine and address the causes for the non-implementation of the projects and expedite its implementation. If the project is no longer necessary or needed recommend for the reversion of the allocated funds for subsequent appropriation to other important and achievable projects in order to attain the desired socio-economic development.
observations.managementAction: AAR appointed an Assistant Municipal Engineer to expedite the preparation of the Program of Works and the implementation of the projects. The Sangguniang Bayan issued a Resolution for the reversion of unutilized appropriations from FY 2007 to 2016 to Unappropriated Surplus.
observations.reason: As of report, the AAR required the Municipal Development Council to prioritize the much needed projects to barangays. Some activities were implemented and funds reallocated; however majority of continuing appropriations still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management create and strengthen the GAD Focal Point System, establish the GAD database, formulate and implement GADs Code and submit the Accomplishment Report, in accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular No.2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The LGU created the GAD Focal Point System, establish the GAD database and submitted the GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for CYs 2017 and 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management execute Job Order individually and provide the specific job descriptions or details and on the expected outputs in order to determine the expected necessity of hiring Job Order personnel.
observations.managementAction: AAR issued Job Order/Contract individually to personnel hired from July to December, 2018.
observations.reason: Job Order/Contract were issued individually but some required documentation/details of outputs still lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer or the Designated Disbursing Officer exercise due care in the release of payments for every transaction and to stamp “PAID” on the supporting payrolls, vouchers and supporting documents. Likewise, ensure that the cashbooks and supporting documents are properly updated and reconciled.
observations.managementAction: The designated Disbursing Officer “PAID” on the supporting payrolls, DVs and supporting documents. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant exercise due care and conducted periodic reconciliations.
observations.reason: Though the LGU stamped “PAID” for the disbursement vouchers, the supporting payrolls, DVs and supporting documents were not completely filled up and not all stamped.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct physical inventory of Supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment and render a report thereon duly categorized by type of supplies and property, plant and equipment. Also require the Accounting Unit and Treasurer’s Office to maintain required Supplies/PPE Ledger Cards and Stock/Property Cards.
observations.managementAction: Inventory committee was created and conducted physical inventory in 2015, 2016 and 2017. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office already maintain stock cards.
observations.reason: Not all required properties were reported in the RPCPPE for CY 2017. Some records were updated while others still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit a duly certified list or Tax Rolls showing all the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to be prepared by the Municipal Assessor and submitted to the Municipal Treasurer for recording in the RPTAR and in turn submit to the Municipal Accountant a list of Taxpayers for recording the RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts pursuant to Section 248, Chapter 2, Title Two of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Assessor already recorded RPTAR for 1st quarter of 2017 only.
observations.reason: Not all taxpayers list were submitted; only 1st quarter 2017 RPTAR prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer conduct intensive tax campaign, employ collection strategies, and institute legal remedies pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA 7160 in order to persuade taxpayers to pay soon. Also, conduct auction sale of taxable real property in the Assessor’s Office, transfer of ownership and the new owner of the property could not be located.
observations.managementAction: AAR conducted tax campaign to 24 barangays. The PTO is now on the process of instituting legal remedies and of instituting an auction sale of taxable real property in the Assessor’s Office. Transfer of ownership documents and the new owner of the property could not be located.
observations.reason: Collections increased to ₱463,437.19 or 132% more than its target of ₱350,000.00, however there was a delay in instituting legal remedies and auction sale.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require the Philippine Councilor’s League, Vice Mayor’s League and League of Municipalities of the Philippines organized by elected officials, to issue a government official receipt (Accountable Form No. 51) for all collections made so that proper financial accountability could be established. Further, ensure that all future payments to these leagues for their conventions, membership dues and registration fees be receipted with a government official receipt which is AF No. 51.
observations.managementAction: AAR required the leagues to present a government official receipt for their conventions and membership dues and other collections. Also, ensure future payments be receipted with AF No. 51.
observations.reason: The leagues were requested to present official receipts, however the leagues did not issue government official receipts for prior collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from making appropriations and disbursements for PPAs not in accordance with DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 1998. Likewise we identified recommended that management fast track the implementation of those PPAs identified as priority needs and appropriated in the said budget.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board based their appropriation and budget preparation on the PPAs and implement accordingly. Management fast tracked the implementation of the identified PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management fast track the implementation of LDRRMF projects/activities to achieve the purpose for which the fund was appropriated. Instruct the DRRMO to monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF and prepare LDRRMFIP indicating the list of priority PPAs for the unexpended balance of prior years using the prescribed format.
observations.managementAction: AAR already hired a permanent MDRRMO who will focus on the implementation of the projects. The LGU conducted monitoring activities.
observations.reason: The LGU spent P6,286,622.64 for PPAs as of Aug., 2018 and hired MDRRMO; however LDRRMFIP still being prepared and some expenditures still charged to continuing appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all insurable properties be insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to secure thereof from possible untoward effects of acts of nature, accidents and other fortuitous events that may occur on the said properties.
observations.managementAction: All government vehicles were already insured with the GSIS.
observations.reason: Buildings were not yet insured due to the absence of appropriation for the purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record and adjust immediately in the books all reconciling items found to be valid. Transfer the amounts of stale checks to accounts payable or unappropriated surplus as provided for in Section 83(b) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual and Section 97 of PD 1445. Likewise, require the Accounting Unit and Treasurer’s Office to conduct a regular reconciliation of their books.
observations.managementAction: Recording of transactions in the Cashbooks weekly. Transfer and reconciliation conducted. Recording in the cashbook were updated to October 2018.
observations.reason: Collections were deposited and cashbooks were updated; reconciliations of accounts were conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and all accountable officers to strictly comply with regulations on the reporting and remittance of collections, and the Municipal Treasurer be regularly update the cashbooks. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant should conduct periodic reconciliation of the cash accounts. Any difference of both records shall be immediately reconciled and corresponding adjustments be made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Accounting Office were instructed to exercise due care in the daily remittance and reporting. Periodic reconciliations were conducted.
observations.reason: Collections were deposited; recording in the cashbooks were updated but discrepancies remained which were being reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit to the audit team copies of all contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days after its perfection/approval. Likewise, ensure complete submission of procurement documents as required in the IRR of R.A. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR submitted copies of contracts and five (5) days after its perfection. The LGU coordinated and provided necessary procurement documents.
observations.reason: Some POs/Contracts were submitted but not within the prescribed five (5) days; some procurement documentation still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned management officials strictly comply with the documentary requirements on the grant of cash advances for payroll fund as provided for in Items 1.1 and 1.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and attached the certification stating that the previous cash advance has been fully liquidated/accounted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from granting similar allowances, incentives and benefits to individuals in the absence of any legal or statutory basis. Likewise, strictly observe the limitation for the appropriation and expenditures for personal services as provided for in Section 325(a) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR required the LGU to take extra effort to identify and refund the amounts received for unauthorized allowances and incentives. Also, refrain from granting same without legal basis.
observations.reason: No final decision on the motion for reconsideration as of report date; some recoveries are pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management adopt stricter procedures in allowing only mandatory deductions from salaries accruing to their employees so that their monthly net take home pay will not be reduced to an amount lower than their net take home pay of P4,000.00, as provided in Section 37 of RA 10155 or the General Appropriations Act 2012.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will limit the granting of loan to employees with net take home pay below P4,000.00. Continuous deduction of payroll loans.
observations.reason: While controls were tightened, continuous deductions of payroll loans still resulted in net take home pay below P4,000.00 for some employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Government to institute appropriate measures for the recovery and/or settlement of this claim, and to locate the documents to prove validity of the claim. Otherwise, send a letter for write-off to COA Adjudication and Settlement Board after exerting all necessary measures.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will take extra effort to locate documents to prove validity of the claim, otherwise, send a letter for write-off to Adjudication Board.
observations.reason: Absence of supporting documents to locate the validity of the claim; request for write-off filed to Adjudication Board and awaiting final action; no disclosure made in FS by Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
In order to present a reliable balance of the Cash in Bank account at
year end, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to verify the reconciling items and to record the adjusting or
correcting journal entries. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should be
reminded to prepare the reconciliation with the end objective of reporting an
accurate balance of the account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require all accountable
officials and employees of the LGU with outstanding cash advances to
immediately settle their cash advances by submitting the necessary documents
as proof of the disbursement made or thru cash refund of the unused cash
advances. If request for liquidation remained not acted, we recommend for
the issuance of demand letter. If it is still not acted by the accountable officer,
appropriate legal action shall be filed against the employees. For employees
who already have transferred to other government agency, seek assistance
from the respective agency head by sending the demand letter thru them.
Also, exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of those who have retired or
out of service, and cause the liquidation of their outstanding cash advance.
This recommendation is without prejudice with the issuance of Notice
of Suspension or Disallowance.

We recommend that all parcels of land owned by the Municipality be
registered under the name of the Municipality for its protection from third
party claims. To facilitate the titling of the lots, we further recommend that
funds be allocated for the purpose.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the Intra-Agency Accounts, “Due from Other Funds
and Due to Other Funds” to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances
of the two accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if appropriate, to
bring the affected accounts into an agreement.

We recommend that Management (a) immediately liquidate the funds
received from the National Government Agencies through submission of
utilization report; (b) return the unexpended balance of completed projects to
the source agencies in compliance with Section 4.9 and Section 6.7 of COA
Circular No. 94-013 dated December 19, 1994; (c) fast track the
implementation of the programs/projects in order to serve the purpose for
which the funds were released to the Municipality and; (e) submit copy/ies of
the MOA for each fund received that serve as a guide in the implementation
of the projects.

We recommend that the Local School Board require all heads of schools
of the municipality to actively participate in the preparation of the budget of
SEF. Also, strictly implement the provisions DepEd, DBM, DILG JC No. 1, s.
2017.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of
Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from the post-audit of transactions.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to transfer the unexpended/unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for
CY 2017 amounting to ₱1,873,773.64to the Special Trust Fund, and the total
amount of ₱1,302,714.53 for FYs 2011 and 2012 not fully utilized after five (5)
years be reverted back to the general fund and be made available for other
social services to be identified by the local sanggunian in accordance to Section
21 of RA 10121.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board prepare the
amended 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan compliant with the
requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003, and implement the plan to
ensure efficient disposal of Municipality’s solid wastes.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials of the
Municipality, to establish the Local Council for the Protection of Children
(LCPC) and allocate one (1) percent of the IRA in the LGUs annual budget in
compliance with Republic Act 9344. It is further recommended that LCPC
shall propose and implement programs, projects and activities allowed under
DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommend that management determine and address the causes for
the non-implementation of the projects and expedite its implementation. If the
project is no longer necessary or needed by the municipality, recommend for
the reversion of the allocated funds for subsequent appropriation to other
important and achievable projects in order to attain desirable socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes as envisioned in
Section 476 of RA 7160 and DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of
Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from the post-audit of transactions.

We recommend that management create and strengthen the GAD Focal
Point System, establish the GAD Database, formulate and implement GADs
Code and submit the Accomplishment Report, in accordance with PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. This
recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommend that management execute Job Order individually and
provide the specific job descriptions or details on the expected outputs in order
to determine the necessity of hiring Job Order personnel.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or the Designated
Disbursing Officer exercise due care in the release of payments for every
transaction and to stamp “PAID” on the payrolls, vouchers and its supporting
documents upon release of payment to comply with established rules and
regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should submit a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as the basis of the report. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant to record the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable and the contra accounts of Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income in adherence to Section 20 of NGAS Manual, Volume I. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the book balance of the said accounts with the actual receivables.
observations.managementAction: See related discussion in Finding No. 3, Part II of this report. No certified list of taxpayers was submitted by the Municipal Treasurer for CY 2016.
observations.reason: No certified list of taxpayers for CY 2016 was submitted by the Municipal Treasurer; hence receivables remained unrecorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Government to institute appropriate measures for the recovery and/or settlement of this amount and to locate the documents to prove validity of the claim. Otherwise, send a letter for write-off to COA Adjudication and Settlement Board after exerting all the necessary measures.
observations.reason: No concrete action was initiated by management on outstanding receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management conduct physical inventory of
Supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment and render a report thereon duly
categorized by type of the supplies and property, plant and equipment. We
also recommend that the required Supplies/PPE Ledger Cards of the
accounting unit and Stock/Property Cards of the property unit, be properly
and completely maintained for purposes of reconciliation pursuant to the
pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 111 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management (a) keep on file a valid MOA for each
funds received as a guide in the effective implementation of the project; (b)
enforce liquidation of the funds received from the National Government
Agencies through submission of liquidation reports in accordance with COA
Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; (c) fast track implementation of
the projects since funds are readily available in order not to deprive the
intended beneficiaries of its immediate use; and (d) return to the Source
Agency the balance of unutilized fund for projects already completed to enable
the national government to reprogram the funds for other felt-need programs/
projects.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting
entries for the unrecorded RPT and SET Receivables. Henceforth, a duly
certified list or Tax Rolls showing all the names of taxpayers and the amount
due and collectible for the year should be prepared by the Municipal Assessor
and submitted to the Municipal Treasurer for recording in the RPTAR and in
turn submit to the Municipal Accountant a list of Taxpayers for recording the
RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts pursuant to Section 248,
Chapter 2, Title Two of RA 7160.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer conduct intensive tax
campaign, employ collection strategies, and institute legal remedies pursuant
to the pertinent provisions of RA 7160 in order to persuade the Taxpayers to
pay their tax on time.

We recommend that the salary schedule for CY 2017 be revised
adopting the rates of a 3rd class municipality or its prevailing income
classification, and henceforth, strictly observe the existing guidelines on the
computation of compensation adjustments for its officials and employees
including the salary step increment.

We recommend that management strictly implement the prescribed
guidelines of the Mondragon Educational Assistance Program as contained in
Municipal Ordinance No 07 series of 2015 in order to attain its objective of
helping the poor but deserving students.

We recommend that management require the Philippine Councilor’s
League, Vice Mayor’s League of the Philippines and League of Municipalities
of the Philippines organized by elected officials, to issue a government official
receipt (Accountable Form No. 51) for all collections made so that proper
financial accountability could be established. Further, ensure that all future
payment to these leagues for membership dues, registration fees and others be
receipted with a government official receipt which is AF No. 51.

We recommend that management refrain from making appropriations
and disbursements for PPAs not in accordance with DECS-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular No. 01, series of 1998. Likewise we recommend that management fast
track the implementation of those PPAs identified as priority needs that are
appropriated in the said budget for the immediate use of the intended
beneficiaries.

We recommend that management fast track the implementation of
projects/activities under LDRRMF to achieve the purpose for which the fund
was appropriated. Instruct the DRRMO to monitor and evaluate the
utilization of LDRRMF to ensure full implementation of pre-disaster
preparedness programs and limit the fund charges to expenditures supportive
of disaster risk management activities pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002
and to prepare LDRRMFIP indicating the list of priority PPAs for the
unexpended balance of prior years using the prescribed format.

We recommend that management comply with the provisions of RA
656. It should insure all its insurable physical assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to secure thereof from possible untoward effects
of acts of nature, accidents and other fortuitous events that may occur on the
said properties.

We recommend that management should workout for an approved 10-
year solid waste management plan which may be updated every year pursuant
to the provisions of Sections 16 and 17 of RA 9003. Likewise, we recommend
that management strictly implement the Solid Waste Management Plan and
enforce the other relevant provisions of the said law in order to attain a
systematic and comprehensive solid waste management for the protection of
public health and the environment.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and
Accountant to observe strictly Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 of
COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of accounts and financial
reports.

We recommend that management implement immediately the
unimplemented projects under the 20% DF and henceforth, implement within
the timeframe as scheduled in the Annual Investment Plan all development
projects consistent with the general policy of the government and mandated
responsibility of the LGU officials as embodied in Joint DBM-DILG
Memorandum Circular 2011-1. Likewise, avoid the charging of expenditure
items not covered by the fund in order to attain desirable socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record and adjust immediately in the books all reconciling items found to be valid. Transfer the deposits for CY 2013 to 2016 payable or unappropriated surplus and in SEF – unrecorded collections in the amount of P52,024.58 for CY 2014 to 2016. Likewise, require the Accounting Unit and Treasurer’s Office to conduct a regular reconciliation of their books.
observations.managementAction: Accountant and Municipal Treasurer showed conducted reconciling items in the quarterly reconciliation of accounts. Transfer the deposits for CY 2013 to 2016 unrecorded collections in the amount of P52,024.58 for CY 2014 to 2016.
observations.reason: Bank reconciliation statements still showed reconciling items in the GF and TF – unrecorded collections for CY 2014 to 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the grant of additional cash advances to those with previous outstanding cash advances, refrain from accepting partial liquidations, and exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of those who have retired or out of service, and cause the liquidation of their outstanding cash advance.
observations.managementAction: AAR sent demand letters to accountable officers and copy furnished the Audit Team. In case of non-compliance, instruct the Accounting Office to withhold the salaries of concerned officials and employees as provided in Item 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002. Likewise, end of contract and not anymore settled their balance by deduction to payroll. The total outstanding Advances for Officers and Employees accounts for 2012 increased by P30,064,302.88 as compared to the balance last 2011. Some of the accountable officers were already retired, and not in the LGU.
observations.reason: Additional cash advances were still allowed by management. The accountable employees settled their outstanding cash advances for 2013 by deduction to their payroll. Payrolls and accounts for 2012 were still not settled; some accountable officers were already retired or out of service.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct physical inventory of supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment and render a report thereon duly categorized by type of the supplies and property, plant and equipment. We also recommend that the required Supplies/PPE Ledger Cards of the accounting unit and Stock/Property Cards of the property unit, be properly and completely maintained for purposes of reconciliation pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 111 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR conducted physical inventory of properties except for the land owned by the LGU. Management indicated that some equipment were not included in the inventory report because of lack of information of its valuation and the amount of properties in the inventory report do not reconcile with the amount recorded in the books of accounts. Very old and small value properties were very hard to locate and the subsidiary ledgers which were prepared by the previous accountant for property, plant and equipment accounts have no details/description.
observations.reason: See related discussion in Finding No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe Section 7 of RA 9679 wherein the premium contributions of the municipality to the PAG-IBIG Fund should not exceed the limit of P100.00 per employee per month. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor make representations with the HDMF to consider the over-remittance of PAG-IBIG Premiums as advance payment on premium contributions of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer limited the employer’s contribution for PAG-IBIG to P100.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and all accountable officers to strictly comply with regulations on the reporting and remittance of collections, and the Municipal Treasurer regularly update the cashbooks. Likewise, any difference between both records should be immediately reconciled and the corresponding adjustment should be made to affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and bank reconciliation was regularly update the cashbooks. Any difference should be immediately reconciled and corresponding adjustment should be made to affected accounts.
observations.reason: Cashbooks were submitted for audit. Also, conducted daily reconciliation of cash collections and deposits were not turned over each day, and the deposits of collections to depository bank were still not intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to observe Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of accounts and financial reports.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required accounts and reports for CY 2015. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Suspension/Disallowance on the unsubmitted accounts and/or results of post-audit of transactions.
observations.reason: See related discussion in AAR 2015. The average number of days of delay per month reduced to 95 days in CY 2016 from 106 days in the previous year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of COA Circulars No. 2012-002 and 2014-002. Moreover, we require the submission of the necessary documents in the payment for various supplies and medicines and hauling services, and the reports on the receipt distribution/issuance and other necessary documents/supporting documents relative to relief goods received from DSWD RO8. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to reclassify the amount of purchased goods and medicines charged outright to expense account to the appropriate relief goods inventory account. We also recommend preparation of LDRRMFIP for MDRRMF CY 2016 indicating the unexpended balances.
observations.managementAction: AAR submitted the necessary documents for CY 2015 to 2016. Some DVs were for distribution and issuance of relief goods received from DSWD RO8 in CY 2015 were not submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: See related discussion in AAR for CY 2014 to 2016. LDRRMFIP for the year covers the MOOE and QRF for CY 2014 to 2016. The unexpended balance of LDRRMF CY 2016 totaling P1,983,211.74 was transferred from General Fund to Trust Fund on December 2016. Some DVs obligated under the fund were not supported with necessary documents such as canvass sheets, BAC Resolution, copies of contracts, and purchase orders.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to submit to the audit team copies of all contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days after its perfection/approval. Likewise, we require the complete submission of the procurement documents outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of R.A. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Submitted copies of contracts and purchase orders for CY 2016. Also submitted the paid disbursement vouchers of such contracts and orders. The submission of the documents forming part thereof was not within five (5) days from its perfection/approval.
observations.reason: Submission of contracts/purchase orders for CY 2016 was delayed/not within five (5) days of perfection/approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare and submit to the auditor the AAPSI within 60 days from receipt of the audit report copy furnished the agencies pursuant to the provision of Section 93 of RA 10651 to ensure that all recommendations are implemented and to provide enough time for the auditor to validate the same.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the AAPSI for the year ended December 31, 2015 on December 29, 2016.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of AAPSI. AAPSI for audit observations and recommendations for the year ended December 31, 2015 was submitted on December 29, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the concerned management officials strictly comply with the documentary requirements on the grant of cash advances for payroll fund as provided for in Items 1.1 and 1.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Also, require certification from the Accountant that the previous cash advances have been liquidated/accounted.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required payrolls and certifications. Some previous cash advances were settled.
observations.reason: Still cash advances for salaries, wages, allowances and the like were not supported with payrolls and a certification from the Accountant that the previous cash advance have been liquidated/accounted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management enforce Section 287 of RA 7160 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1. Likewise, properly plan and schedule all development procurement activities to pave the way for timely implementation and completion thereof in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction: The LGU appropriated the amount P25,934,220.09 for CY 2016 AIP. Submitted the amount of pending projects from previous years and some projects were implemented in CY 2017. AAR noted that some projects were implemented in CY 2017.
observations.reason: There is a delay in preparation of program of works, plan and etc. due to lack of manning in the Engineering Office, and there were projects from previous years prioritized in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately revert the unutilized PDAF to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund of the National Treasury pursuant to the Supreme Court’s decision.
observations.managementAction: Deposited the unutilized PDAF amount to the account of Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the officials and employees concerned to refund the amounts received for unauthorized allowances. Henceforth, refrain from granting similar allowances, incentives and benefits to individuals in the absence of any legal or statutory basis. Likewise, strictly observe the limitation for the appropriation and expenditures for personal services as provided for in Section 325(a) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Submitted Notice of Appeal to COA Region and Appeal from Central Office. Do not issue the Certificate of Net Take Home Pay to employees who have lower net take home pay that their monthly net have lower than required loans and tend to auto renew.
observations.reason: There is delay in the decision of Notice of Appeal to COA Central Office. Some employees stopped the unauthorized allowances and benefits after the issuance of Notices.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adopt stricter procedures in allowing only mandatory deductions from salaries accruing to their employees so that their monthly net take home pay will not be reduced to an amount lower than Four Thousand Pesos (P4,000.00), as provided in Section 37 of RA 10155 or the General Appropriations Act 2012.
observations.managementAction: Do not issue the Certificate of Net Take Home Pay to employees who have lower net take home pay than required under GAA.
observations.reason: The monthly amortizations existed from previous years and there were employees who had lower net take home pay due to existing loans and auto-renewals.
